trivia_bullets.text: Convenient Target is an Aura that suspects the enchanted creature on entry — making that creature simultaneously +1/+1 stronger and unable to block while it has menace. The card's name encodes the noir murder-mystery logic of MKM: in a whodunit, the 'convenient target' is the one framed because they're available, not because they're actually guilty. [Scryfall]

Convenient Target has a graveyard recursion ability ({2}{R}: return this card from your graveyard to your hand), making it repeatable across a game. The Suspect keyword it applies (menace + can't block) is a persistent effect as long as the creature remains suspected. In combination with the +1/+1 bonus, the card packages aggression and recurring utility into a single Aura. No flavor text on this card. [Scryfall]

Art by Gaboleps (a single-name credited artist) depicts a horned figure cupping a fiery orb in which a tiny armored humanoid is visible — the 'convenient target' made literally small and helpless inside the trap. The implied narrative (a demon-like figure with complete control over a miniaturized victim) maps cleanly onto the card's mechanical role: making a creature maximally threatening while simultaneously vulnerable. [Scryfall]
